udt_struct_v0.doc: Claim tracking structure for each user-token pair
udt_struct_v0.name: Claim

fields.doc: Cumulative amount claimed by the user for this token
fields.name: amount

fields.doc: Merkle root that was active when the last claim occurred
fields.name: merkle_root

fields.doc: Ledger sequence when the last claim occurred
fields.name: timestamp

tuple_v0.doc: Notice: Tracks cumulative claimed amounts for each user and token\nDev: Maps user => token => Claim details (amount, timestamp, merkleRoot)
tuple_v0.name: Claimed

tuple_v0.doc: Notice: Trusted addresses authorized to update the Merkle root\nDev: Maps address => trusted status (1 = trusted, 0 = not trusted)
tuple_v0.name: CanUpdateMerkleRoot

tuple_v0.doc: Note: Authorization for operators to claim on behalf of users\nDev: Maps user => operator => authorization status (1 = authorized, 0 = not authorized)\nDev: When operator is None, it enables any address to claim for the user
tuple_v0.name: Operators

tuple_v0.doc: Note: Custom recipient addresses for user claims per token\nDev: Maps user => token => recipient (None = default for all tokens)\nDev: Setting recipient for None token sets the default recipient for all tokens
tuple_v0.name: ClaimRecipient

tuple_v0.doc: Note: Global operators authorized to claim specific tokens on behalf of any user\nDev: Maps operator => token => authorization (1 = authorized, 0 = not authorized)\nDev: Authorization for None token allows claiming any token for any user
tuple_v0.name: MainOperators

tuple_v0.doc: Note: Pending collateral refunds when dispute transfer could not be delivered\nDev: Maps recipient => token => amount
tuple_v0.name: PendingRefund

void_v0.doc: Storage schema version for upgrade migrations (see `StorageMigrationArgs`).
void_v0.name: ContractVersion

function_v0.doc:
Claims rewards for a set of users based on Merkle proofs
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address initiating the claim (must be user or authorized operator)
* `users` - Addresses claiming rewards (or being claimed for)
* `tokens` - ERC20 tokens being claimed
* `amounts` - Cumulative amounts earned (not incremental amounts)
* `proofs` - Merkle proofs validating each claim
# Notes
* Users can only claim for themselves unless they've authorized an operator
* Arrays must all have the same length
function_v0.name: claim

udt_struct_v0.doc: Merkle tree structure containing the root hash and optional IPFS reference
udt_struct_v0.name: MerkleTree

fields.doc: Optional IPFS hash of the complete tree data (deprecated but kept for compatibility)
fields.name: ipfs_hash

fields.doc: Root of a Merkle tree whose leaves are (address user, address token, i128 amount)\nrepresenting the cumulative amount of tokens earned by each user
fields.name: merkle_root

function_v0.doc: Returns the current active Merkle tree\n# Returns\n* `Option<MerkleTree>` - The current tree with merkle root and IPFS hash, or None if not set
function_v0.name: get_tree

function_v0.doc:
Extends the TTL of the contract instance, code, and key persistent entries
# Notes
* Callable by anyone \xe2\x80\x94 keeping the contract alive is a public good
* Extends instance storage (which includes all Instance entries and the contract code)
* User-specific persistent entries have their TTL extended individually when accessed
function_v0.name: extend_ttl

function_v0.doc:
Returns the claim details for a specific user and token
# Arguments
* `user` - The user address to query
* `token` - The token address to query
# Returns
* `Option<Claim>` - Claim details including amount, timestamp, and merkle root, or None if no claim exists
function_v0.name: get_claimed

function_v0.doc:
Reverts to the previous Merkle tree immediately
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
* Cannot be called if there's an active dispute (must resolve dispute first)
function_v0.name: revoke_tree

function_v0.doc:
Updates the active Merkle tree with new reward data
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be trusted address or governor)
* `merkle_root` - New Merkle root containing updated reward information
* `ipfs_hash` - IPFS hash of the complete tree data
# Notes
* Can only be called by trusted addresses or governor
* Trusted addresses cannot update during an active dispute period to prevent circumventing disputes
* Saves the current tree to lastTree before updating
function_v0.name: update_tree

function_v0.doc:
Creates a dispute to freeze the current Merkle tree update
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address creating the dispute
* `reason` - Explanation for why the tree update is being disputed
# Notes
* Requires depositing `dispute_amount` of `dispute_token` as collateral
* Can only be called within the dispute period after a tree update
* Deposit is slashed if dispute is rejected, returned if valid
function_v0.name: dispute_tree

function_v0.doc: Returns the address of the current disputer, or None if no active dispute
function_v0.name: get_disputer

function_v0.doc:
Returns whether an operator is authorized to claim on behalf of a user
# Arguments
* `user` - The user address to query
* `operator` - The operator address to query (None = any operator)
# Returns
* `bool` - True if operator is authorized, false otherwise
function_v0.name: get_operator

function_v0.doc:
Initializes the contract with access control manager
# Arguments
* `access_control_manager` - Address of the access control manager contract
# Notes
* Sets up the contract for reward distribution operations
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc: Returns the previous Merkle tree (for rollback purposes)\n# Returns\n* `Option<MerkleTree>` - The last tree before the most recent update, or None if not set
function_v0.name: get_last_tree

function_v0.doc:
Recovers SEP-41 tokens accidentally sent to the contract
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `token_address` - Address of the token to recover
* `to` - Address that will receive the recovered tokens
* `amount_to_recover` - Amount of tokens to recover
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
function_v0.name: recover_sep41

function_v0.doc:
Returns whether an address is trusted to update the Merkle root
# Arguments
* `address` - The address to query
# Returns
* `bool` - True if address is trusted, false otherwise
function_v0.name: get_is_trusted

function_v0.doc:
Toggles an address's authorization to update the Merkle tree
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `trust_address` - Address whose trusted status is being toggled
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
* Trusted addresses can update trees
function_v0.name: toggle_trusted

function_v0.doc:
Returns the currently active Merkle root for claim verification
# Returns
* `BytesN<32>` - The Merkle root that is currently valid for claims
# Notes
* Returns lastTree.merkle_root if within the dispute period or if there's an active dispute
* Returns tree.merkle_root if dispute period has passed and there's no active dispute
function_v0.name: get_merkle_root

function_v0.doc:
Resolves an ongoing dispute
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `valid` - True if the dispute is valid (tree reverted, deposit returned to disputer)
False if invalid (deposit sent to governor, dispute period extended)
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
function_v0.name: resolve_dispute

function_v0.doc:
Toggles an operator's authorization to claim rewards on behalf of a user
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address toggling the authorization (must be user or governance)
* `user` - User granting or revoking the authorization
* `operator` - Operator address being authorized or deauthorized (None = any address)
# Notes
* If operator is None, allows any address to claim for the user
* If operator is Some, authorizes that specific operator
* Only the user themselves or governance can toggle operator status
function_v0.name: toggle_operator

udt_struct_v0.doc: Arguments for [`UpgradeableMigratable::migrate`] after a WASM upgrade.
udt_struct_v0.name: StorageMigrationArgs

function_v0.doc: Returns the dispute token address
function_v0.name: get_dispute_token

function_v0.doc:
Returns whether an operator is a main operator for a token
# Arguments
* `operator` - The operator address to query
* `token` - The token address to query (None = all tokens)
# Returns
* `bool` - True if operator is authorized as main operator, false otherwise
function_v0.name: get_main_operator

function_v0.doc:
Updates the token required as collateral for disputes
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `dispute_token` - New dispute token address
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
* Cannot be changed while there is an active dispute
function_v0.name: set_dispute_token

function_v0.doc: Returns the dispute amount required to create a dispute
function_v0.name: get_dispute_amount

function_v0.doc: Returns the dispute period in epoch units
function_v0.name: get_dispute_period

function_v0.doc:
Returns the epoch duration used for timing calculations
# Returns
* `u32` - The current epoch duration in seconds
# Notes
* Returns custom epoch duration if set, otherwise returns default EPOCH_DURATION (3600 seconds)
function_v0.name: get_epoch_duration

function_v0.doc: Returns any pending refund amount for a recipient-token pair
function_v0.name: get_pending_refund

function_v0.doc:
Updates the amount of tokens required to create a dispute
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `dispute_amount` - New dispute amount
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
* Cannot be changed while there is an active dispute
function_v0.name: set_dispute_amount

function_v0.doc:
Updates the dispute period duration
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `dispute_period` - Number of epochs to wait before a tree update becomes effective
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
function_v0.name: set_dispute_period

function_v0.doc:
Updates the epoch duration used for timing calculations
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
* `epoch_duration` - New epoch duration in seconds
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
function_v0.name: set_epoch_duration

function_v0.doc:
Returns the custom claim recipient for a user and token
# Arguments
* `user` - The user address to query
* `token` - The token address to query (None = default for all tokens)
# Returns
* `Option<Address>` - The recipient address, or None if no custom recipient is set
function_v0.name: get_claim_recipient

function_v0.doc:
Sets a custom recipient address for a user's token claims
# Arguments
* `caller` - User setting their claim recipient
* `recipient` - Address that will receive claimed tokens (None = default to user)
* `token` - Token for which to set the recipient (None = all tokens)
# Notes
* Users can override this recipient when calling claim_with_recipient
* If token is None, sets the default recipient for all tokens
* If token is Some, sets the recipient for that specific token
* If recipient is None, removes the custom recipient setting
function_v0.name: set_claim_recipient

function_v0.doc:
Claims a previously undeliverable dispute refund for the caller
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address claiming the pending refund
* `token` - Token for which the pending refund was recorded
# Notes
* If token transfer still fails, pending refund remains claimable for a later retry
function_v0.name: claim_pending_refund

function_v0.doc:
Claims rewards with custom recipient addresses and callback data
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address initiating the claim
* `users` - Addresses claiming rewards (or being claimed for)
* `tokens` - ERC20 tokens being claimed
* `amounts` - Cumulative amounts earned (not incremental amounts)
* `proofs` - Merkle proofs validating each claim
* `recipients` - Custom recipient addresses for each claim (None = use default)
* `datas` - Arbitrary data passed to recipient's onClaim callback (if recipient is a contract)
# Notes
* Only caller claiming for themselves can override the recipient address
* Non-None recipient addresses override any previously set default recipients
function_v0.name: claim_with_recipient

function_v0.doc:
Permanently disables contract upgradeability
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be governor)
# Notes
* Only callable by governor
* This action is irreversible - use with extreme caution
function_v0.name: revoke_upgradeability

function_v0.doc: Returns the persisted storage schema version (`0` if unversioned legacy state).
function_v0.name: contract_schema_version

function_v0.doc: Returns the timestamp at which the current dispute period ends
function_v0.name: get_end_of_dispute_period

function_v0.doc: Returns the AccessControlManager contract address\n# Returns\n* `Address` - The AccessControlManager contract address
function_v0.name: get_access_control_manager

function_v0.doc:
Points this contract at a new AccessControlManager (ACM).
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be a governor in both ACMs)
* `new_access_control_manager` - Address of the ACM to trust going forward
# Notes
* The caller must be governor according to the current ACM stored on this contract.
* The caller must also be governor in `new_access_control_manager`.
* Governor-set parity (all old ACM governors exist in the new ACM) is enforced on-chain.
* Migration flow: call old ACM `set_access_control_manager(...)` first, then call this setter.
function_v0.name: set_access_control_manager

function_v0.doc:
Toggles a main operator's authorization to claim tokens on behalf of any user
# Arguments
* `caller` - Address calling the function (must be guardian or governor)
* `operator` - Operator whose status is being toggled
* `token` - Token for which authorization applies (None = all tokens)
# Notes
* Only callable by guardian for an individual token or governor if it's for all tokens
* Main operators can claim for any user without individual user authorization
* If token is None, the operator can claim any token for any user (requires governor)
* If token is Some, the operator can claim that specific token (requires guardian)
function_v0.name: toggle_main_operator_status

function_v0.doc: Returns whether upgradeability is deactivated\n# Returns\n* `bool` - True if upgradeability is deactivated, false otherwise
function_v0.name: get_upgradeability_deactivated

cases.doc: When migration is attempted but not allowed due to upgrade state.
cases.name: MigrationNotAllowed

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when the merkle root is set.
event_v0.name: SetRoot

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when an index is claimed.
event_v0.name: SetClaimed

cases.doc: The merkle root is not set.
cases.name: RootNotSet

cases.doc: The provided index was already claimed.
cases.name: IndexAlreadyClaimed

udt_union_v0.doc: Storage keys for the data associated with `MerkleDistributor`
udt_union_v0.name: MerkleDistributorStorageKey

void_v0.doc: The Merkle root of the distribution tree
void_v0.name: Root

tuple_v0.doc: Maps an index to its claimed status
tuple_v0.name: Claimed

cases.doc: The merkle proof length is out of bounds.
cases.name: MerkleProofOutOfBounds

cases.doc: The index of the leaf is out of bounds.
cases.name: MerkleIndexOutOfBounds

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when the contract is paused.
event_v0.name: Paused

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when the contract is unpaused.
event_v0.name: Unpaused

cases.doc: The operation failed because the contract is paused.
cases.name: EnforcedPause

cases.doc: The operation failed because the contract is not paused.
cases.name: ExpectedPause

udt_union_v0.doc: Storage key for the pausable state
udt_union_v0.name: PausableStorageKey

void_v0.doc: Indicates whether the contract is in paused state.
void_v0.name: Paused
